1. Read the full contents of these compound component files:

   - `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`
   - `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`

2. Look for patterns including:
   - How React context is created and used
   - How `data-slot` attributes are applied
   - How `cn()` is used for className merging
   - How prop types are defined (React.ComponentProps)
   - How function declarations are structured
   - How `cva` (class-variance-authority) is used if at all
   - How `data-state` and `data-orientation` attributes are handled
   - The "use client" directive placement

3. Also read `apps/v4/lib/utils.ts` to understand the cn() utility.

Report all findings in detail, including exact code patterns and conventions.

1	import * as React from "react"
2	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
3	import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
4	
5	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  "inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      variant: {
12	        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90",
13	        destructive:
14	          "bg-destructive text-white hover:bg-destructive/90 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:bg-destructive/60 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40",
15	        outline:
16	          "border bg-background shadow-xs hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:border-input dark:bg-input/30 dark:hover:bg-input/50",
17	        secondary:
18	          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80",
19	        ghost:
20	          "hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:hover:bg-accent/50",
21	        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
22	      },
23	      size: {
24	        default: "h-9 px-4 py-2 has-[>svg]:px-3",
25	        xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-md px-2 text-xs has-[>svg]:px-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
26	        sm: "h-8 gap-1.5 rounded-md px-3 has-[>svg]:px-2.5",
27	        lg: "h-10 rounded-md px-6 has-[>svg]:px-4",
28	        icon: "size-9",
29	        "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-md [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
30	        "icon-sm": "size-8",
31	        "icon-lg": "size-10",
32	      },
33	    },
34	    defaultVariants: {
35	      variant: "default",
36	      size: "default",
37	    },
38	  }
39	)
40	
41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),

221	)
222

Perfect! I've successfully read all four files. Let me now provide you with a comprehensive analysis of how shadcn/ui builds compound components with React context.

## Detailed Findings Report

### 1. **cn() Utility Function** (`apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`)

The `cn()` utility is a simple but powerful composition of two libraries:

```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

**How it works:**
- Takes variadic arguments of type `ClassValue[]`
- `clsx()` merges class name arrays and objects, handling truthy/falsy values
- `twMerge()` intelligently merges Tailwind classes, resolving conflicts (last defined class wins)
- **Pattern**: Allows safe merging of base styles with user-provided `className` props

---

### 2. **Carousel Component** (`carousel.tsx`)

#### "use client" Directive
- **Placement**: Line 1, top of file
- **Reason**: Component uses `useEmblaCarousel` hook and React context, requiring client-side rendering

#### React Context Pattern

**Context Creation (lines 33):**
```typescript
const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)
```

**Context Type Definition (lines 24-31):**
- `CarouselContextProps` is a union type combining:
  - Carousel state: `carouselRef`, `api`, `scrollPrev()`, `scrollNext()`, `canScrollPrev`, `canScrollNext`
  - Original props: Spread of `CarouselProps`
- **Null default**: Allows error detection via custom hook

**Custom Hook Pattern (lines 35-43):**
```typescript
function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```

**Provider Pattern (lines 107-132):**
- Carousel component wraps children with `CarouselContext.Provider`
- Value object includes all state and callbacks needed by child components
- Provider updates on dependency changes (api, setApi via useEffect)

#### Function Declarations
```typescript
function Carousel({
  orientation = "horizontal",
  opts,
  setApi,
  plugins,
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CarouselProps)
```

**Pattern**: 
- Destructures known props first
- Spreads `...props` at end
- Type union: `React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CarouselProps`

#### data-slot Attributes
- **Line 126**: `data-slot="carousel"` on root div
- **Line 142**: `data-slot="carousel-content"` on content wrapper
- **Line 163**: `data-slot="carousel-item"` on item element
- **Line 184**: `data-slot="carousel-previous"` on button
- **Line 214**: `data-slot="carousel-next"` on button

**Purpose**: Semantic data attributes for testing, styling hooks, or component identification

#### cn() Usage Pattern
```typescript
className={cn(
  "base-styles",
  orientation === "horizontal" ? "horizontal-styles" : "vertical-styles",
  className
)}
```

**Pattern**: 
- Base styles first
- Conditional styles based on component state
- User-provided className last (allows override)

#### Data Attributes for State
- **data-orientation**: Not explicitly used in this component
- Instead uses context value: `orientation` consumed via `useCarousel()`
- State management via `useEmblaCarousel` hook and `React.useState()`

---

### 3. **Tabs Component** (`tabs.tsx`)

#### "use client" Directive
- Line 1, top of file
- Uses `cva` from class-variance-authority and Radix UI primitives

#### CVA (Class-Variance-Authority) Pattern

**CVA Definition (lines 28-41):**
```typescript
const tabsListVariants = cva(
  "group/tabs-list inline-flex w-fit items-center justify-center rounded-lg p-[3px] text-muted-foreground group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:h-9 group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:h-fit group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:flex-col data-[variant=line]:rounded-none",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "bg-muted",
        line: "gap-1 bg-transparent",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
    },
  }
)
```

**CVA Pattern Benefits:**
- Centralizes variant logic
- Type-safe variant props via `VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>`
- Base styles + variant overrides
- Used to generate className via `tabsListVariants({ variant })`

#### Tabs Root Component (lines 9-26)

```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>)
```

**Notable Pattern:**
- Uses Radix UI `TabsPrimitive.Root` instead of custom React context
- Passes `data-orientation={orientation}` to underlying primitive
- Uses parent `group/tabs` selector in child classes

#### TabsTrigger Complex Styling (lines 59-76)

The TabsTrigger demonstrates advanced className merging:
```typescript
className={cn(
  "relative inline-flex h-[calc(100%-1px)] flex-1 items-center justify-center gap-1.5 ...",
  "group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:bg-transparent group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:data-[state=active]:bg-transparent ...",
  "data-[state=active]:bg-background data-[state=active]:text-foreground ...",
  "after:absolute after:bg-foreground after:opacity-0 after:transition-opacity ...",
  className
)}
```

**Advanced Patterns:**
- **Group selectors**: `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:w-full` - parent data attributes affect children
- **Pseudo-element selectors**: `after:absolute`, `after:opacity-0` - style ::after pseudo-elements
- **State selectors**: `data-[state=active]:`, `data-[state=closed]:` - respond to Radix state
- **Descendant selectors**: `[&_svg]:pointer-events-none` - style all SVG children
- **Dark mode**: `dark:text-muted-foreground` - conditional dark mode classes
- Multiple cn() calls for complex conditional logic

#### data-slot Attributes
- **Line 16**: `data-slot="tabs"` on root
- **Line 51**: `data-slot="tabs-list"` on list
- **Line 65**: `data-slot="tabs-trigger"` on trigger
- **Line 84**: `data-slot="tabs-content"` on content

#### data-variant & data-orientation
- **Line 17**: `data-orientation={orientation}` passed to primitive
- **Line 52**: `data-variant={variant}` set on list based on prop
- Used in selector chains: `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:flex-col`

#### Type Safety with VariantProps
```typescript
function TabsList({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.List> &
  VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>)
```

**Pattern**: Union type combining:
- Primitive component props: `React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.List>`
- CVA variant props: `VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>`

---

### 4. **Accordion Component** (`accordion.tsx`)

#### "use client" Directive
- Line 1, top of file

#### Radix UI Primitive Wrapper Pattern

Unlike Carousel (custom context), Accordion wraps Radix UI primitives:

```typescript
function Accordion({
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Root>) {
  return <AccordionPrimitive.Root data-slot="accordion" {...props} />
}
```

**Pattern**: Minimal wrapper
- Passes through all props via `...props`
- Adds only `data-slot` attribute
- Delegates state management to Radix UI primitive

#### Compound Component Structure

**Four components:**
1. **Accordion** (root)
2. **AccordionItem** - wrapper for each accordion section
3. **AccordionTrigger** - clickable header
4. **AccordionContent** - collapsible content

#### Nesting Pattern

AccordionTrigger wraps children in a Header primitive:
```typescript
function AccordionTrigger({
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Trigger>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Header className="flex">
      <AccordionPrimitive.Trigger
        data-slot="accordion-trigger"
        className={cn(
          "flex flex-1 items-start justify-between gap-4 ...",
          "hover:underline focus-visible:border-ring ...",
          "[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180",
          className
        )}
        {...props}
      >
        {children}
        <ChevronDownIcon className="..." />
      </AccordionPrimitive.Trigger>
    </AccordionPrimitive.Header>
  )
}
```

**Advanced Pattern:**
- `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` - rotate SVG when parent has `data-state=open`
- Direct child selector: `>svg`
- CSS attribute selector: `[data-state=open]`

#### data-state Attributes
- **Line 58**: `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up` - animation when closed
- **Line 58**: `data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down` - animation when open
- Automatically set by Radix UI based on accordion state

#### data-slot Attributes
- **Line 12**: `data-slot="accordion"` on root
- **Line 21**: `data-slot="accordion-item"` on item
- **Line 36**: `data-slot="accordion-trigger"` on trigger
- **Line 57**: `data-slot="accordion-content"` on content

#### Animation Pattern
```typescript
className="overflow-hidden text-sm data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down"
```

- Uses Tailwind animations triggered by `data-state` attribute
- Assumes animations are defined in Tailwind config

---

## Summary of Key Patterns

### 1. **"use client" Placement**
- Always at the top of file (line 1)
- Required for components using hooks, context, or event handlers

### 2. **Context Creation Patterns**
- **Carousel**: Custom context with `null` default for error detection
- **Tabs**: Uses Radix UI context (no custom context needed)
- **Accordion**: Uses Radix UI context (no custom context needed)

### 3. **Type Definition Patterns**
```typescript
// Carousel: Union type with null
React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

// Props: Union of component props and custom props
React.ComponentProps<typeof Button> & CarouselProps

// CVA: Variant props
VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>
```

### 4. **Function Declaration Pattern**
```typescript
function ComponentName({
  specificProp,
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CustomProps)
```

### 5. **cn() Usage Pattern**
```typescript
cn(
  "base-classes",
  state ? "conditional-classes" : "alternative-classes",
  className // user override last
)
```

### 6. **data-slot Attributes**
- Consistently named: `data-slot="component-name"`
- Used on every component for identification/testing
- Example values: "carousel", "tabs", "accordion", "carousel-item", "tabs-trigger"

### 7. **data Attributes for State**
- **data-orientation**: "horizontal" | "vertical"
- **data-variant**: Custom variant names
- **data-state**: "open" | "closed" (Radix UI automatic)
- Used in selectors: `data-[state=active]:`, `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:`

### 8. **CVA Usage** (Tabs only)
- Centralizes variant logic
- Provides type-safe variant props
- Can be exported for consumer use: `export { tabsListVariants }`

### 9. **Selector Patterns**
- **Group selectors**: `group/name` + `group-data-[attr]/name:`
- **Pseudo-elements**: `after:`, `before:`, `[&::after]:`
- **Child selectors**: `[&_child]:`, `>svg`
- **Attribute selectors**: `[data-state=active]:`, `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:`
- **Dark mode**: `dark:classname`

---

## File Paths

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`

```typescript
function AccordionTrigger({
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Trigger>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Header className="flex">
      <AccordionPrimitive.Trigger
        data-slot="accordion-trigger"
        className={cn(
          "flex flex-1 items-start justify-between gap-4 ...",
          "hover:underline focus-visible:border-ring ...",
          "[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180",
          className
        )}
        {...props}
      >
        {children}
        <ChevronDownIcon className="..." />
      </AccordionPrimitive.Trigger>
    </AccordionPrimitive.Header>
  )
}
```

**Advanced Pattern:**
- `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180` - rotate SVG when parent has `data-state=open`
- Direct child selector: `>svg`
- CSS attribute selector: `[data-state=open]`

#### data-state Attributes
- **Line 58**: `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up` - animation when closed
- **Line 58**: `data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down` - animation when open
- Automatically set by Radix UI based on accordion state

#### data-slot Attributes
- **Line 12**: `data-slot="accordion"` on root
- **Line 21**: `data-slot="accordion-item"` on item
- **Line 36**: `data-slot="accordion-trigger"` on trigger
- **Line 57**: `data-slot="accordion-content"` on content

#### Animation Pattern
```typescript
className="overflow-hidden text-sm data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down"
```

- Uses Tailwind animations triggered by `data-state` attribute
- Assumes animations are defined in Tailwind config

---

## Summary of Key Patterns

### 1. **"use client" Placement**
- Always at the top of file (line 1)
- Required for components using hooks, context, or event handlers

### 2. **Context Creation Patterns**
- **Carousel**: Custom context with `null` default for error detection
- **Tabs**: Uses Radix UI context (no custom context needed)
- **Accordion**: Uses Radix UI context (no custom context needed)

### 3. **Type Definition Patterns**
```typescript
// Carousel: Union type with null
React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

// Props: Union of component props and custom props
React.ComponentProps<typeof Button> & CarouselProps

// CVA: Variant props
VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>
```

### 4. **Function Declaration Pattern**
```typescript
function ComponentName({
  specificProp,
  className,
  children,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CustomProps)
```

### 5. **cn() Usage Pattern**
```typescript
cn(
  "base-classes",
  state ? "conditional-classes" : "alternative-classes",
  className // user override last
)
```

### 6. **data-slot Attributes**
- Consistently named: `data-slot="component-name"`
- Used on every component for identification/testing
- Example values: "carousel", "tabs", "accordion", "carousel-item", "tabs-trigger"

### 7. **data Attributes for State**
- **data-orientation**: "horizontal" | "vertical"
- **data-variant**: Custom variant names
- **data-state**: "open" | "closed" (Radix UI automatic)
- Used in selectors: `data-[state=active]:`, `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:`

### 8. **CVA Usage** (Tabs only)
- Centralizes variant logic
- Provides type-safe variant props
- Can be exported for consumer use: `export { tabsListVariants }`

### 9. **Selector Patterns**
- **Group selectors**: `group/name` + `group-data-[attr]/name:`
- **Pseudo-elements**: `after:`, `before:`, `[&::after]:`
- **Child selectors**: `[&_child]:`, `>svg`
- **Attribute selectors**: `[data-state=active]:`, `[&[data-state=open]>svg]:`
- **Dark mode**: `dark:classname`

---

## File Paths

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the patterns. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.

## Detailed Findings: shadcn/ui Codebase Patterns

Based on my exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here are the key patterns and structural insights:

### 1. **Simple Component Structure (Button & Separator)**

**Button** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button.tsx`):
- Uses `cva` (class-variance-authority) for variant management
- Includes multiple variants: `variant` (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and `size` variants
- Uses `Slot` from radix-ui for `asChild` polymorphism pattern
- Sets `data-slot`, `data-variant`, and `data-size` attributes for styling hooks
- Comprehensive styling includes focus states, disabled states, hover states, dark mode support
- Handles SVG icon sizing with `[&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4`

**Separator** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`):
- Wraps `Separator` primitive from radix-ui
- Uses `orientation` prop (horizontal/vertical)
- Uses `data-orientation` attribute selector for responsive styling:
  ```
  data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full 
  data-[orientation=vertical]:h-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px
  ```
- Sets `data-slot="separator"` for CSS targeting
- Decorative by default

### 2. **No Existing Stepper Component**

Search for "stepper" returned **zero results**. Only chart-related files with "step" exist (chart-line-step, chart-area-step), not a UI stepper component. This confirms there's no duplication risk.

### 3. **Data-Orientation Pattern Usage**

The codebase extensively uses `data-orientation` for orientation-aware components:

**Files using `data-orientation` include:**
- toggle-group.tsx
- scroll-area.tsx
- field.tsx
- button-group.tsx
- tabs.tsx

**Pattern in ToggleGroup** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/toggle-group.tsx`):
```typescript
<ToggleGroupPrimitive
  data-slot="toggle-group"
  data-variant={variant}
  data-size={size}
  data-spacing={spacing}
  data-orientation={orientation}
  style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
  className={cn(
    "flex w-fit flex-row items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] 
     data-vertical:flex-col data-vertical:items-stretch",
    className
  )}
  {...props}
>
```

Uses CSS custom properties for dynamic spacing and data attribute selectors for layout changes.

### 4. **Data-State Pattern Usage**

The codebase uses `data-state` for component state management. Common state values:
- `data-state="open"` / `data-state="closed"` (collapsible, accordion)
- `data-state="checked"` / `data-state="unchecked"` (checkboxes)
- `data-state="active"` (tabs, active states)
- `data-state="selected"` (data tables, selections)

**Tailwind CSS Custom Variants** defined in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/packages/shadcn/src/tailwind.css`:
```css
@custom-variant data-open {
  &:where([data-state="open"]),
  &:where([data-open]:not([data-open="false"])) {
    @slot;
  }
}

@custom-variant data-horizontal {
  &:where([data-orientation="horizontal"]) {
    @slot;
  }
}

@custom-variant data-vertical {
  &:where([data-orientation="vertical"]) {
    @slot;
  }
}
```

This allows using utility classes like `data-open:rotate-180`, `data-horizontal:flex-row`, `data-vertical:flex-col`.

### 5. **Common Tailwind CSS Layout Classes in Compound Components**

**Flexbox & Layout:**
- `flex` - base flexbox container
- `flex-row` / `flex-col` - direction
- `items-center` / `items-start` / `items-stretch` - alignment
- `justify-center` / `justify-between` - justification
- `w-full` / `w-fit` - width utilities
- `gap-X` / `gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))]` - spacing between items
- `shrink-0` - prevent shrinking
- `flex-1` - grow to fill space
- `flex-wrap` - wrap content

**Specific Patterns:**
- `inline-flex` - inline block behavior with flexbox
- `grid place-content-center` - center content in grid
- `relative` / `absolute` - positioning for overlays
- `transition-all` - smooth transitions
- `data-horizontal:flex-row` - conditional layout based on orientation

**From ToggleGroup example:**
```css
flex w-fit flex-row items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] 
data-vertical:flex-col data-vertical:items-stretch
```

### 6. **Component Architecture Patterns**

**Pattern: Compound Components with Context**

ToggleGroup demonstrates context usage:
```typescript
const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
    spacing?: number
    orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  }
>({...})

// Parent provides context
<ToggleGroupPrimitive>
  <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing, orientation }}>
    {children}
  </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
</ToggleGroupPrimitive>

// Children consume context
function ToggleGroupItem(...) {
  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
  // Use context.orientation for layout decisions
}
```

**Pattern: Data Attributes for Styling**

All components use `data-slot` attributes:
```typescript
<SelectTrigger
  data-slot="select-trigger"
  data-size={size}
  className={cn(...)}
/>
```

**Pattern: CVA (Class Variance Authority) for Variants**

Used extensively for managing component variants:
```typescript
const buttonGroupVariants = cva(
  "cn-button-group flex w-fit items-stretch",
  {
    variants: {
      orientation: {
        horizontal: "...",
        vertical: "flex-col ...",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      orientation: "horizontal",
    },
  }
)
```

**Pattern: The `cn` Utility Function**

(`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/lib/utils.ts`):
```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```
Combines clsx for conditional classes and twMerge for Tailwind conflict resolution.

### 7. **Component Slot Naming Convention**

Components use consistent `data-slot` naming:
- `data-slot="button"`
- `data-slot="separator"`
- `data-slot="toggle-group"`
- `data-slot="toggle-group-item"`
- `data-slot="input-group"`
- `data-slot="input-group-addon"`
- `data-slot="breadcrumb"`
- `data-slot="breadcrumb-item"`

This enables CSS targeting at all nesting levels using attribute selectors.

### 8. **Example: ButtonGroup Structure**

Good reference for orientation-based compound component (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/button-group.tsx`):
- Root component sets `data-orientation` and applies variant styling
- Child components use conditional styling based on parent orientation
- Separator component adjusts orientation to match parent
- Uses `*:data-slot` selectors to target all slotted children

### 9. **Key Dependencies Used**

- `@base-ui/react` - Unstyled component primitives
- `class-variance-authority` - Variant management (CVA)
- `clsx` - Conditional class combination
- `tailwind-merge` - Conflict resolution for Tailwind classes
- `radix-ui` - Accessible primitives (used in some components)

### 10. **Compound Component Best Practices Observed**

1. Always export all sub-components (Root, Item, Trigger, Content, etc.)
2. Use context to pass shared configuration down the tree
3. Add `data-slot` attributes to every component for CSS targeting
4. Use CVA for variant management when applicable
5. Support custom className extensions via `cn()` utility
6. Use `data-*` attributes for state and orientation
7. Set up CSS custom properties for dynamic values (e.g., `--gap`)
8. Implement proper TypeScript typing for all component props
9. Always support spreading remaining props with `{...props}`

---

**Summary for Stepper Implementation:**
Based on these patterns, a stepper component should:
- Use orientation prop (horizontal/vertical) with `data-orientation` attribute
- Use context to pass step information down to children
- Use `data-state` for step states (pending, current, completed, error)
- Follow the slot naming: `stepper`, `stepper-item`, `stepper-trigger`, etc.
- Use CVA for styling variants
- Leverage Tailwind CSS gap and flex utilities for layout
- Use CSS custom properties for step spacing and sizing

- `data-slot="toggle-group-item"`
- `data-slot="input-group"`
- `data-slot="input-group-addon"`
- `data-slot="breadcrumb"`
- `data-slot="breadcrumb-item"`

This enables CSS targeting at all nesting levels using attribute selectors.

### 8. **Example: ButtonGroup Structure**

Good reference for orientation-based compound component (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/button-group.tsx`):
- Root component sets `data-orientation` and applies variant styling
- Child components use conditional styling based on parent orientation
- Separator component adjusts orientation to match parent
- Uses `*:data-slot` selectors to target all slotted children

### 9. **Key Dependencies Used**

- `@base-ui/react` - Unstyled component primitives
- `class-variance-authority` - Variant management (CVA)
- `clsx` - Conditional class combination
- `tailwind-merge` - Conflict resolution for Tailwind classes
- `radix-ui` - Accessible primitives (used in some components)

### 10. **Compound Component Best Practices Observed**

1. Always export all sub-components (Root, Item, Trigger, Content, etc.)
2. Use context to pass shared configuration down the tree
3. Add `data-slot` attributes to every component for CSS targeting
4. Use CVA for variant management when applicable
5. Support custom className extensions via `cn()` utility
6. Use `data-*` attributes for state and orientation
7. Set up CSS custom properties for dynamic values (e.g., `--gap`)
8. Implement proper TypeScript typing for all component props
9. Always support spreading remaining props with `{...props}`

---

**Summary for Stepper Implementation:**
Based on these patterns, a stepper component should:
- Use orientation prop (horizontal/vertical) with `data-orientation` attribute
- Use context to pass step information down to children
- Use `data-state` for step states (pending, current, completed, error)
- Follow the slot naming: `stepper`, `stepper-item`, `stepper-trigger`, etc.
- Use CVA for styling variants
- Leverage Tailwind CSS gap and flex utilities for layout
- Use CSS custom properties for step spacing and sizing

I need to design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in a shadcn/ui component library. This is a READ-ONLY planning task — do not write any files.

## Codebase Context

The codebase is a shadcn/ui v4 registry. All UI components live in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`. I've thoroughly studied the existing patterns:

### Key Pattern: Carousel (custom React context compound component)
Located at `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx`:
- `"use client"` at top
- Creates `CarouselContext = React.createContext<...| null>(null)` 
- Custom hook `useCarousel()` with error throw if null
- Root component wraps children with `CarouselContext.Provider`
- All sub-components call `useCarousel()` to access shared state
- Function declarations (not arrow functions)
- `React.ComponentProps<"div">` for prop types
- `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` for className merging
- `data-slot="carousel"`, `data-slot="carousel-item"`, etc.
- Named exports at bottom: `export { Carousel, CarouselContent, ... }`

### Key Pattern: Tabs (Radix wrapper with CVA)
Located at `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`:
- Sets `data-orientation={orientation}` on root
- Uses `data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col` pattern in Tailwind
- Uses `group/tabs` for group selectors
- Uses `data-[state=active]:` for state-based styling
- CVA for variant management with `VariantProps`

### Key Pattern: Separator
Located at `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`:
- `data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full data-[orientation=vertical]:h-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px`

### Utils
`apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`: `cn()` = `twMerge(clsx(inputs))`

### Registry Entry Formats

**UI entry** (`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema"
export const ui: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion",
    type: "registry:ui",
    dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
    files: [{ path: "ui/accordion.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }],
  },
  // ... alphabetically ordered
]
```

**Example entry** (`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema"
export const examples: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion-demo",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["accordion"],
    files: [{ path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }],
  },
]
```

### Demo File Pattern
- Demos use `"use client"` when they have useState
- Import from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component-name`
- Export a default function like `export default function ComponentDemo()`
- Use Button from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button` for interactive controls

## Requirements

Create a Stepper compound component with these exports:

1. **`Stepper`** — root container
   - Props: `orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"` (default "horizontal"), `activeStep: number`, className, div props
   - React context shares `activeStep` and `orientation`
   - `<div>` with `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation`, flex layout

2. **`StepperItem`** — wrapper per step
   - Props: `step: number`, className, div props
   - `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-step`, `data-state` ("completed" | "active" | "pending")
   - Needs its own context to share `step` number with children (StepperTrigger needs it)

3. **`StepperTrigger`** — clickable circle/number
   - Props: className, button props
   - `data-slot="stepper-trigger"`
   - Shows step number by default, uses children if provided
   - Styled differently based on data-state

4. **`StepperTitle`** — label
   - Props: className, p props
   - `data-slot="stepper-title"`

5. **`StepperDescription`** — description
   - Props: className, p props
   - `data-slot="stepper-description"`

6. **`StepperSeparator`** — line between steps
   - Props: className, div props
   - `data-slot="stepper-separator"`
   - Horizontal or vertical line based on orientation
   - Visual state changes when NEXT step is completed

7. **`StepperContent`** — content panel
   - Props: `step: number`, className, div props
   - Only renders when `step === activeStep`
   - `data-slot="stepper-content"`

## Design Task

Please produce a detailed implementation plan covering:

1. **File structure** — exactly which files to create/modify
2. **Context design** — the StepperContext type and StepperItemContext type, the custom hooks
3. **Component-by-component implementation** — for each of the 7 components:
   - Exact prop type definition
   - Element rendered
   - data attributes
   - Tailwind classes (using shadcn design tokens like `bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`, `border`, `text-muted-foreground`, etc.)
   - How it reads from context
4. **Demo file** — full structure of the stepper-demo.tsx
5. **Registry entries** — exact entries to add

Focus on getting the Tailwind styling right for a polished look:
- Active step trigger: filled circle with `bg-primary text-primary-foreground`
- Completed step trigger: filled with a checkmark or similar
- Pending step trigger: outlined with `border border-input text-muted-foreground`
- Separator: `bg-border` when pending, `bg-primary` when completed
- Use `size-8` or `size-10` for the trigger circle
- Use `text-sm` for titles, `text-xs text-muted-foreground` for descriptions

Consider the two-context approach:
- `StepperContext` for global state (activeStep, orientation)
- `StepperItemContext` for per-item state (step number) so StepperTrigger can know which step it belongs to

45	  )
46	}
47	
48	export { Badge, badgeVariants }

1	import * as React from "react"
2	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
3	import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
4	
5	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  "inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      variant: {
12	        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90",
13	        destructive:
14	          "bg-destructive text-white hover:bg-destructive/90 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:bg-destructive/60 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40",
15	        outline:
16	          "border bg-background shadow-xs hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:border-input dark:bg-input/30 dark:hover:bg-input/50",
17	        secondary:
18	          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80",
19	        ghost:
20	          "hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:hover:bg-accent/50",
21	        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
22	      },
23	      size: {
24	        default: "h-9 px-4 py-2 has-[>svg]:px-3",
25	        xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-md px-2 text-xs has-[>svg]:px-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
26	        sm: "h-8 gap-1.5 rounded-md px-3 has-[>svg]:px-2.5",
27	        lg: "h-10 rounded-md px-6 has-[>svg]:px-4",
28	        icon: "size-9",
29	        "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-md [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
30	        "icon-sm": "size-8",
31	        "icon-lg": "size-10",
32	      },
33	    },
34	    defaultVariants: {
35	      variant: "default",
36	      size: "default",
37	    },
38	  }
39	)
40	
41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }
65

Good, `lucide-react` is available. Now I have everything needed to produce a comprehensive implementation plan. --- ## Detailed Implementation Plan: Stepper Compound Component ### 1. File Structure Three files need to be created, and two existing files need to be modified: **New files:** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` -- the main component file - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` -- the demo example **Files to modify (append entries):** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` -- add stepper UI entry - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` -- add stepper-demo example entry --- ### 2. Context Design Two React contexts are needed, following the two-context pattern. **StepperContext** (global, provided by `Stepper` root): ```typescript type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" } const StepperContext = React.createContext(null) function useStepper() { const context = React.useContext(StepperContext) if (!context) { throw new Error("useStepper must be used within a ") } return context } ``` **StepperItemContext** (per-item, provided by `StepperItem`): ```typescript type StepperItemContextProps = { step: number state: "completed" | "active" | "pending" } const StepperItemContext = React.createContext(null) function useStepperItem() { const context = React.useContext(StepperItemContext) if (!context) { throw new Error("useStepperItem must be used within a ") } return context } ``` The state derivation logic lives in `StepperItem`: it compares `step` (its own prop) to `activeStep` (from `StepperContext`): - `step < activeStep` produces `"completed"` - `step === activeStep` produces `"active"` - `step > activeStep` produces `"pending"` --- ### 3. Component-by-Component Implementation #### 3.1 `Stepper` (root container) **Props:** ```typescript type StepperProps = { activeStep: number orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" } function Stepper({ activeStep, orientation = "horizontal", className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) ``` **Renders:** A `
` wrapping children with `StepperContext.Provider`. **Data attributes:** - `data-slot="stepper"` - `data-orientation={orientation}` **Tailwind classes:** ``` "group/stepper flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col" ``` The `group/stepper` class enables descendant components to style themselves based on the stepper's orientation using `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/stepper:` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:` prefixes, exactly matching the pattern used by `tabs` (`group/tabs`). **Context provided:** `{ activeStep, orientation }` --- #### 3.2 `StepperItem` (per-step wrapper) **Props:** ```typescript function StepperItem({ step, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { step: number }) ``` **Renders:** A `
` wrapping children with `StepperItemContext.Provider`. **State derivation** (inside the component body): ```typescript const { activeStep } = useStepper() const state = step < activeStep ? "completed" : step === activeStep ? "active" : "pending" ``` **Data attributes:** - `data-slot="stepper-item"` - `data-step={step}` - `data-state={state}` **Tailwind classes:** ``` "group/stepper-item flex items-center gap-2 group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:flex-col" ``` Rationale: In horizontal mode, the item is a horizontal row (step indicator + separator). In vertical mode, items stack vertically. The `group/stepper-item` class allows children (trigger, title, description, separator) to respond to the item's state. **Context provided:** `{ step, state }` --- #### 3.3 `StepperTrigger` (clickable circle/number) **Props:** ```typescript function StepperTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"button">) ``` **Reads from context:** - `useStepperItem()` to get `step` and `state` **Renders:** A `
) } ``` **Design note on StepperSeparator and StepperContent placement:** There is an architectural decision here. The `StepperSeparator` lives between `StepperItem` elements as a sibling (not inside `StepperItem`). This means `StepperSeparator` cannot access `StepperItemContext`. The separator needs to know whether it represents a completed connection. Since it sits between step N and step N+1, the cleanest approach is to have the separator accept no `step` prop and instead derive its state from position. However, this adds complexity. **Revised approach:** Place `StepperSeparator` inside `StepperItem` as a child. This way it accesses `StepperItemContext` naturally. The demo becomes: ```
Account Create your account
Profile Set up your profile
Complete Review and confirm
``` This way the separator inside `StepperItem` for step 0 knows it is "completed" when `step 0 < activeStep`, which is exactly the correct visual behavior (the line between step 0 and step 1 fills in once you move past step 0). Similarly, `StepperContent` should also be placed outside `Stepper` OR accept its own `step` prop and read `activeStep` from `StepperContext`. Since the requirements specify `StepperContent` has a `step: number` prop, it should read `useStepper()` for `activeStep` and conditionally render. It can live either inside or outside the `Stepper` root -- but inside is more natural so it can access the context. The demo wraps everything within `Stepper`: ``` {/* Step indicators row */} ... ... ... {/* Content panels */} ... ... ... ``` Wait -- this creates a layout issue. The `Stepper` root is a flex row (horizontal), so `StepperContent` children would be placed inline. The solution: the demo wraps the step indicators in a separate container, or the `Stepper` root uses a more flexible layout. Let me reconsider. **Final layout approach:** The `Stepper` root is the flex container for step items only. `StepperContent` components are placed **outside** the `Stepper` root in the demo, but they still need access to `StepperContext`. This means either: (a) `StepperContent` receives `activeStep` as a regular prop (no context needed), or (b) We wrap everything in a higher-level provider. Given the requirements state `StepperContent` has `step: number` as a prop and renders conditionally when `step === activeStep`, the simplest and most idiomatic approach is **(a)**: `StepperContent` does not use context at all. It simply accepts `step` and the consumer controls visibility externally. But that would require the consumer to do `{activeStep === 0 &&
...
}` which is what `StepperContent` encapsulates. The cleanest design: **`StepperContent` lives inside the `Stepper` provider, and the `Stepper` root layout accommodates it.** The `Stepper` root can use `flex-col` wrapping and contain both the step indicator row and the content below: ```typescript // Stepper renders:
{children}
``` Then the step indicator row is provided by the consumer wrapping `StepperItem` elements in their own div: ```
... ... ...
... ... ... ``` However, this pushes layout responsibility to the consumer. A better pattern: **The `Stepper` root is just a context provider with a minimal wrapper div.** It does NOT impose flex-row or flex-col -- the consumer handles layout. This is how Radix `Tabs.Root` works (the flex layout is configured by the consumer or via minimal defaults). Looking at the tabs component: `Tabs` uses `flex gap-2 data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col`. So it IS flex-col for horizontal orientation (list on top, content below). **Final decision on Stepper root layout:** For horizontal orientation: The root should be `flex flex-col gap-4` (step indicators on top, content below). For vertical orientation: The root should be `flex flex-row gap-4` (step indicators on the left, content on the right). But this gets complex. The simplest and most flexible approach is to keep the root as a plain wrapper (no directional flex) and let `StepperItem` elements handle their own layout. The demo author handles the overall layout. This gives maximum flexibility. Actually, let me re-read the requirements: the `Stepper` spec says "flex layout." Let me keep it simple: ``` "group/stepper flex data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col" ``` And `StepperContent` is placed outside the `Stepper` in the demo, receiving `activeStep` directly as a prop instead of from context: **Revised `StepperContent`:** ```typescript function StepperContent({ step, activeStep, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { step: number; activeStep: number }) ``` Actually, this is getting overengineered. Let me go back to the requirements as stated: `StepperContent` has `step: number` and renders when `step === activeStep`. The cleanest way: `StepperContent` uses `useStepper()` context, so it must be inside the provider. The `Stepper` root div is the flex row/col for step indicators. The consumer places `StepperContent` outside `Stepper` and just does conditional rendering manually, OR we separate the provider from the layout div. **Best architecture -- separate provider from layout:** ```typescript function Stepper({ activeStep, orientation = "horizontal", className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) { return (
{children}
) } ``` Then in the demo, `StepperContent` is rendered inside the `Stepper` but it uses CSS to break out of the flex row. OR, we simply accept that `StepperContent` does not live inside `Stepper` and instead just receives `activeStep` as a prop for simplicity. **Final decision: Keep `StepperContent` simple -- it takes both `step` and `activeStep` as props.** No context dependency. This is the most pragmatic approach: ```typescript function StepperContent({ step, activeStep, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { step: number; activeStep: number }) { if (step !== activeStep) return null return (
{children}
) } ``` Wait -- the requirements clearly say `StepperContent` has props `step: number, className, div props` and "Only renders when `step === activeStep`." There is no `activeStep` prop listed. This implies it reads `activeStep` from context. So it must be inside the `Stepper` provider tree. The simplest resolution: make the `Stepper` component render the context provider **without** wrapping in a layout div, and export a separate layout component, OR use the approach where `Stepper` is just the provider and `StepperItem` elements plus `StepperContent` are all children at the same level. The consumer wraps the step indicators in a flex container themselves. Actually, the simplest and most aligned approach: **The `Stepper` root outputs a `div` that is flex-col, with the step items in a flex-row sub-container auto-created, and content below.** But this means two divs. Or even simpler: the `Stepper` just renders `flex-col` and the consumer puts the step row and content together: ```jsx
Account Profile Complete

Account content here

Profile content here

Complete content here

 ``` With `Stepper` root having no flex direction forcing. This is the best approach. Let me finalize it. --- ### Revised Final Component Specifications #### `Stepper` root **Tailwind classes:** ``` "group/stepper flex flex-col gap-4" ``` The root is always `flex-col`. The horizontal/vertical orientation affects how `StepperItem` elements are arranged internally and how separators render, not the root's direction. The root stacks: step indicator row + content area. But wait, for vertical orientation, you might want steps on the left and content on the right. Let me keep the root minimal and let the consumer decide: ``` "group/stepper" ``` No flex. Just a context provider and a plain div wrapper with `group/stepper`. The consumer controls layout entirely. This matches how `Carousel` just uses `"relative"` and leaves internal layout to sub-components. **Final:** The root is a plain wrapper div: ```typescript className={cn("group/stepper", className)} ``` The consumer is responsible for overall page layout. The step indicator bar and content areas are laid out by the consumer using normal Tailwind utilities on wrapper divs. --- ### 6. Registry Entries **UI entry** to add in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`): ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["lucide-react"], files: [ { path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` Note: `lucide-react` is the only external dependency (for `CheckIcon`). No Radix dependency. No `registryDependencies` since no other registry components are used. **Example entry** to add in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (alphabetically, near other `s` entries): ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` --- ### 7. Summary of Architectural Decisions | Decision | Choice | Rationale | |----------|--------|-----------| | Context pattern | Two contexts (`StepperContext` + `StepperItemContext`) | `StepperTrigger` and `StepperSeparator` need to know which step they belong to without explicit props | | State derivation | Computed in `StepperItem` from `step` vs `activeStep` | Single point of truth; children read derived `state` from `StepperItemContext` | | Root layout | Minimal `group/stepper` wrapper | Maximum consumer flexibility; matches Carousel's approach | | Orientation styling | `data-[orientation=...]` on elements + `group-data-[orientation=...]/stepper:` on children | Matches exact pattern from `tabs.tsx` and `separator.tsx` | | Completed trigger visual | `CheckIcon` from lucide-react | Already used in `checkbox.tsx`, `context-menu.tsx`, `select.tsx` | | Function style | Function declarations (not arrows) | Matches all existing components | | Prop types | `React.ComponentProps<"div">` / `React.ComponentProps<"button">` | Matches carousel, card, and other custom components | | `StepperContent` context usage | Reads `activeStep` from `useStepper()` | Must be inside `Stepper` provider; consumer places it below the step indicator row | ### 8. Potential Challenges 1. **Separator positioning in vertical mode.** The vertical separator needs to align under the trigger circle. Using `ml-4` (half of `size-8 = 32px`, so 16px) centers it. If consumers change the trigger size via className override, the separator alignment may drift. This is acceptable for a v1. 2. **StepperContent placement.** Since `StepperContent` must be inside the `Stepper` provider to access context, but the `Stepper` root is just a plain wrapper, consumers need to structure their JSX to place the step indicator bar and content area appropriately. The demo will show the canonical pattern. 3. **Accessibility.** The stepper should include `role="group"` on the step indicators container and `aria-current="step"` on the active trigger. The `StepperTrigger` button gets `aria-current="step"` when active. This is a straightforward addition to `StepperTrigger`: ```typescript aria-current={state === "active" ? "step" : undefined} ``` 4. **No `onStepChange` callback on root.** The requirements specify `activeStep` as a controlled prop. The consumer manages state externally (via `useState`). This is the correct controlled component pattern. --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` (new file -- the main component) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` (new file -- the demo) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (modify -- add stepper UI entry) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (modify -- add stepper-demo example entry) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393215794-4drvf1/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` (reference -- primary pattern to follow for context + compound component structure)
